title: refund — trait composition
description: Two owned traits. `BelongsToRefund` is the FK trait for downstream models (RefundLine + future marketing-event rows tied to a specific refund). `HasRefundLines` is the inverse-side convenience on the parent Refund model. Order matters when composed with `BelongsToTenant`: tenant MUST come first so subsequent traits observe the tenant context.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `refund_id` FK. Adds the `refund()` belongsTo relation and refuses cross-tenant writes at save time. Applied to RefundLine directly + planned growth::marketing::MarketingEvent (Wave 5) that carries refund_id for negative-value conversion attribution.
usedBy: growth::marketing::MarketingEvent (Wave 5 — marketing events fired on RefundSucceeded carry refund_id for negative-value conversion attribution)

owned.description: Composed onto the Refund model. Adds `->lines()` (hasMany), `->hasProration()` (scope filter refund_type='prorated'), `->totalLineAmount()` (SUM of line.amount_cents for invariant check). Convenience: `$refund->lines_count` for the count.

framework.reason: Refund model only — RefundLine is NOT soft-deletable (immutable pivot rows). Retention pipeline handles hard-delete of refunds post 7y.

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

audit.reason: Every write is compliance-relevant (financial-record 7-year retention). Every state transition on Refund (status, approved_by, rejected_by, processed_at, credit_note_id, transaction_id) + every RefundLine creation is audit-critical.

activity.reason: Refund lifecycle transitions (requested / approved / rejected / cancelled / succeeded / failed) feed the staff-visible activity feed. NOT on RefundLine (immutable pivot row — already covered by parent refund activity log).

refund.notes: Order: BelongsToTenant first. HasRefundLines is the inverse-side convenience. SoftDeletes permits marking a refund as deleted while retaining the audit trail through the retention window. SoftDeletes ONLY functional pre-succeeded — Post-succeeded refunds are immutable + retention-pipeline-managed.

refund_line.notes: NO SoftDeletes — RefundLine is 7y-retained HARD as a pivot row. NO HasActivityLog — already covered by parent Refund activity log. Auditable is required for the 7-year financial audit trail. NO Searchable — refund line lookup is always by (refund_id, invoice_line_id), never full-text search.
